findings.title: Wrong-file handling is only defined for legacy `openspec/config.yaml`, not for env-only settings misplaced into `.specflow/config.yaml`
findings.detail: The spec explicitly requires local-runtime/operator-preference settings placed in `.specflow/config.yaml` to be ignored and to emit a warning naming `.specflow/config.env` as the canonical location. The design and tasks only describe detect-and-warn behavior for shared-policy keys left in `openspec/config.yaml`; they do not define how agent-selection or CLI-path settings would be recognized if someone puts them in the YAML file, nor do they add regression coverage for that scenario. Add a concrete detection/warning strategy and tests for env-only settings in the shared YAML.

findings.title: The borderline shared→local override path is too under-specified to implement and verify cleanly
findings.detail: The spec adds a required shared-default/local-override capability, but the design says no current setting exercises it while the tasks still require an override smoke test. That leaves task 1.5 and 5.5 ambiguous: implementers do not know whether to support a generic YAML-to-env override mechanism, which keys are actually classified as borderline, or what concrete behavior the test should prove. Either identify at least one in-scope borderline setting for this change, or specify the exact generic override mechanism and its testable contract.
